personas.name: Camille Sato
personas.mandate: required Core and provider pack loading, typed participation provenance, deny-by-default loaders, bypass containment, fail-closed behavior
questions: Which single production loader API all behavior-driving cmd/gc and config paths must use, and what test fails on direct config.Load bypasses?
Can strict validation prove Core and provider pack participation from resolved config edges, not path or digest coincidence, before any orders, prompts, formulas, scripts, or API state read behavior?
What degraded or operator-visible path exists when Core integrity fails during live reload, and does it avoid silently continuing with stale behavior?
red_flags: Core absence is only a doctor warning after behavior already loaded
token scanners miss wrapper, alias, function-value, or generated API loader bypasses
a corrupted or partially materialized system pack is accepted because path and digest checks are conflated
personas.questions_text: Which single production loader API all behavior-driving cmd/gc and config paths must use, and what test fails on direct config.Load bypasses?;Can strict validation prove Core and provider pack participation from resolved config edges, not path or digest coincidence, before any orders, prompts, formulas, scripts, or API state read behavior?;What degraded or operator-visible path exists when Core integrity fails during live reload, and does it avoid silently continuing with stale behavior?
personas.red_flags_text: Core absence is only a doctor warning after behavior already loaded;token scanners miss wrapper, alias, function-value, or generated API loader bypasses;a corrupted or partially materialized system pack is accepted because path and digest checks are conflated

personas.name: Oleg Marchetti
personas.mandate: Gastown behavior inventory completeness, execution-level witnesses, cross-repo packcompat, old-to-new traceability, source deletion gate
questions: Does the generated inventory enumerate every requester, detector, notification, prompt, order, mail, nudge, and script trigger removed from Core?
For each moved behavior, what old Gas City witness and final gascity-packs witness prove the same trigger fires through normal resolution from the exact public pin?
How does CI prevent deleting or generalizing a behavior before the gascity-packs landing commit and trace row are present?
red_flags: manifest rows are hand-curated, path-only, or existence-only
behaviors with no old tests quietly require no witness
Gas City consumes copied assets instead of verifying the pinned public checkout
personas.questions_text: Does the generated inventory enumerate every requester, detector, notification, prompt, order, mail, nudge, and script trigger removed from Core?;For each moved behavior, what old Gas City witness and final gascity-packs witness prove the same trigger fires through normal resolution from the exact public pin?;How does CI prevent deleting or generalizing a behavior before the gascity-packs landing commit and trace row are present?
personas.red_flags_text: manifest rows are hand-curated, path-only, or existence-only;behaviors with no old tests quietly require no witness;Gas City consumes copied assets instead of verifying the pinned public checkout

personas.name: Leah Okafor
personas.mandate: doctor fix coordinator atomicity, byte-preserving TOML, concurrency with live controllers, advisory locks, idempotent recovery
questions: Do all doctor --fix paths stage FixIntent objects before mutation and hold a city advisory lock across stage, validate, compare-before-rename, and publish?
When scoped TOML edits cannot preserve comments or unknown fields byte-for-byte outside intended changes, does the fixer refuse rather than rewrite whole files?
What recovery is specified for crashes or concurrent old and new binaries between per-file renames so cities cannot remain half-migrated?
red_flags: cross-file failure-atomicity is claimed from per-file rename semantics
doctor rewrites user imports or local forks without proof they are system-generated
network, lock loss, or live controller races can leave city.toml and runtime state inconsistent
personas.questions_text: Do all doctor --fix paths stage FixIntent objects before mutation and hold a city advisory lock across stage, validate, compare-before-rename, and publish?;When scoped TOML edits cannot preserve comments or unknown fields byte-for-byte outside intended changes, does the fixer refuse rather than rewrite whole files?;What recovery is specified for crashes or concurrent old and new binaries between per-file renames so cities cannot remain half-migrated?
personas.red_flags_text: cross-file failure-atomicity is claimed from per-file rename semantics;doctor rewrites user imports or local forks without proof they are system-generated;network, lock loss, or live controller races can leave city.toml and runtime state inconsistent

personas.name: Anand Krishnaswamy
personas.mandate: zero hardcoded roles in Go and assets, symbolic maintenance_worker binding, SDK self-sufficiency, ZFC judgment containment
questions: After binding indirection, does any Go, prompt asset, script, formula, order, generated help, or API route still branch on dog, Mayor, Maintenance, or another concrete role name?
Can controller-owned SDK operations still run when the configured maintenance worker is renamed or omitted, with no dependency on a user agent entry?
Are role-name allowlists narrow, time-bounded, and failing when compatibility fixtures leak into live behavior?
red_flags: gc.routed_to, mail, nudge, warmup, or theme logic still hardcodes dog or Gastown roles
default binding behavior encodes a Go judgment call instead of pure transport
scanner coverage excludes scripts, overlays, docs, dashboard types, or generated fixtures
personas.questions_text: After binding indirection, does any Go, prompt asset, script, formula, order, generated help, or API route still branch on dog, Mayor, Maintenance, or another concrete role name?;Can controller-owned SDK operations still run when the configured maintenance worker is renamed or omitted, with no dependency on a user agent entry?;Are role-name allowlists narrow, time-bounded, and failing when compatibility fixtures leak into live behavior?
personas.red_flags_text: gc.routed_to, mail, nudge, warmup, or theme logic still hardcodes dog or Gastown roles;default binding behavior encodes a Go judgment call instead of pure transport;scanner coverage excludes scripts, overlays, docs, dashboard types, or generated fixtures

personas.name: Lena Hoffmann
personas.mandate: public Gastown pin integrity, immutable content hash, RepoCacheKey identity, synthetic alias retirement, offline and rollback behavior
questions: Are PublicGastownPackVersion, pins.yaml, registry source, packcompat, and direct cache proof all bound to the same immutable commit and subpath?
Can any stale synthetic alias, embedded bytes, lock refresh, install path, or offline upgrade select retired Gastown or Maintenance content after the public pin lands?
What deployable state and rollback narrative exist across the window between gascity-packs landing and Gas City activation pin update?
red_flags: mutable branch or tag pinning lets fetched behavior drift
cache promotion launders stale bytes without digest verification
offline or rollback paths silently fall back to embedded retired behavior
personas.questions_text: Are PublicGastownPackVersion, pins.yaml, registry source, packcompat, and direct cache proof all bound to the same immutable commit and subpath?;Can any stale synthetic alias, embedded bytes, lock refresh, install path, or offline upgrade select retired Gastown or Maintenance content after the public pin lands?;What deployable state and rollback narrative exist across the window between gascity-packs landing and Gas City activation pin update?
personas.red_flags_text: mutable branch or tag pinning lets fetched behavior drift;cache promotion launders stale bytes without digest verification;offline or rollback paths silently fall back to embedded retired behavior

personas.name: Owen Gallagher
personas.mandate: Core versus Gastown ownership split, retired Maintenance source containment, active discovery classifier, no duplicate active behavior
questions: Is there one classifier API used before config load, install, cache lock, validation, discovery, docs lint, prompt, formula, order, script, and hook enumeration?
Does a city running Core plus public Gastown and no Maintenance preserve Gastown-specific behavior without shared Core conditionals?
Can stale in-tree Gastown or Maintenance directories remain on disk without entering active prompts, formulas, orders, scripts, hooks, or rollback states?
red_flags: independent globs bypass retired-source classification
shared Core assets retain Gastown-specific branches instead of moving them to gascity-packs
duplicate formulas, orders, prompts, or scripts are active during compatibility or rollback
personas.questions_text: Is there one classifier API used before config load, install, cache lock, validation, discovery, docs lint, prompt, formula, order, script, and hook enumeration?;Does a city running Core plus public Gastown and no Maintenance preserve Gastown-specific behavior without shared Core conditionals?;Can stale in-tree Gastown or Maintenance directories remain on disk without entering active prompts, formulas, orders, scripts, hooks, or rollback states?
personas.red_flags_text: independent globs bypass retired-source classification;shared Core assets retain Gastown-specific branches instead of moving them to gascity-packs;duplicate formulas, orders, prompts, or scripts are active during compatibility or rollback

personas.name: Hiroshi Tanabe
personas.mandate: production Core embed removal, non-nil empty bootstrap fs, fixture-only tests, GC_BOOTSTRAP skip containment, hidden dependency inventory
questions: After removing production Core from bootstrap embeds, what compile-time or CI check proves no production path imports the deleted bootstrap Core package?
Are tests that need Core assets using minimal fstest fixtures or the relocated system-pack wrapper, not copied production Core snapshots?
Is GC_BOOTSTRAP=skip narrowed to fixture or no-Core tests and structurally unreachable as a production required-system-pack bypass?
red_flags: nil fs.FS or AssetDir packs/core panics survive after embed removal
test fixture copies drift from shipped Core with no freshness check
hooks, overlays, or prompt tests hide a dependency on internal/bootstrap/packs/core
personas.questions_text: After removing production Core from bootstrap embeds, what compile-time or CI check proves no production path imports the deleted bootstrap Core package?;Are tests that need Core assets using minimal fstest fixtures or the relocated system-pack wrapper, not copied production Core snapshots?;Is GC_BOOTSTRAP=skip narrowed to fixture or no-Core tests and structurally unreachable as a production required-system-pack bypass?
personas.red_flags_text: nil fs.FS or AssetDir packs/core panics survive after embed removal;test fixture copies drift from shipped Core with no freshness check;hooks, overlays, or prompt tests hide a dependency on internal/bootstrap/packs/core

personas.name: Yelena Markovic
personas.mandate: Maintenance to Core runtime state migration, jsonl and archive state, spawn-storm ledgers, non-destructive markers, old/new binary concurrency, downgrade continuity
questions: Are jsonl state, archive remotes and refs, spawn-storm ledgers, order skip lists, and escalation fields migrated or preserved with non-destructive markers?
If an old binary writes legacy Maintenance state after the migration-completed marker, how does the new binary detect and handle divergence without data loss?
What round-trip test covers interrupted archive copy, in-flight push state, duplicate writers, and rollback to an older binary?
red_flags: destination-absent copy races two processes or treats half-copied archives as complete
post-marker old-binary writes are silently ignored
renamed orders break existing skip lists or duplicate pending pushes
personas.questions_text: Are jsonl state, archive remotes and refs, spawn-storm ledgers, order skip lists, and escalation fields migrated or preserved with non-destructive markers?;If an old binary writes legacy Maintenance state after the migration-completed marker, how does the new binary detect and handle divergence without data loss?;What round-trip test covers interrupted archive copy, in-flight push state, duplicate writers, and rollback to an older binary?
personas.red_flags_text: destination-absent copy races two processes or treats half-copied archives as complete;post-marker old-binary writes are silently ignored;renamed orders break existing skip lists or duplicate pending pushes

personas.name: Iris Kowalski
personas.mandate: independently deployable slices, decomposition readiness, prerequisite honesty, exact gates, cross-repo sequencing and test coverage
questions: Can tasks be cut so each slice names concrete files, acceptance gates, cross-repo prerequisites, and a revert or one-way upgrade boundary before merge?
Are open questions truly resolved, or are ownership audits, generated artifacts, and gascity-packs branch availability deferred as hidden blockers?
Does each intermediate commit pass the documented local gates and exercise production loaders rather than copied fixtures or direct config.Load shortcuts?
red_flags: tasks batch pin changes, source deletion, doctor mutation, docs, and activation into one fragile landing
status says decomposition-ready while required generators or public pack commits do not exist
fast unit tests are used as the only proof for loader, doctor, runtime-state, and cross-repo behavior changes

personas.red_flags_text: tasks batch pin changes, source deletion, doctor mutation, docs, and activation into one fragile landing;status says decomposition-ready while required generators or public pack commits do not exist;fast unit tests are used as the only proof for loader, doctor, runtime-state, and cross-repo behavior changes

personas.name: Claire Dubois
personas.mandate: operator upgrade DX, terminology matrix, docs and schema generated artifacts, doctor messages, tutorial integrity
questions: Do docs, doctor output, CLI help, generated references, tutorials, and schema artifacts use the same Core, Gastown, retired Maintenance, and store-maintenance vocabulary?
Is the wording matrix executable enough to distinguish retired pack references from legitimate lowercase maintenance or Dolt/store maintenance terms?
Can a new or upgrading operator follow one narrative from missing orders or stale packs through doctor diagnostics, public pin verification, and recovery?
red_flags: operator-facing behavior changes ship with stale docs or non-release docs debt
wording lint creates noisy false positives and gets suppressed wholesale
doctor diagnostics, tutorials, or generated schema still point operators to Maintenance or in-tree Gastown
